title: Sediment subduction and accretion at the Chilean convergent margin between 35° and 40°S
abstract: Sediment subduction and accretion processes at the Chilean convergent margin between 35° and 40°S are investigated using seismic reflection and refraction data from SPOC and legacy cruises. The study characterizes the accretionary prism structure, sediment input to the trench, and the transition between accretionary and erosional modes along this segment of the Chilean margin.

location.description: Chilean convergent margin 35°–40°S; sediment subduction and accretion study using SPOC and legacy seismic data

seismic_lines.name: SPOC and legacy MCS profiles — Chile margin 35°–40°S (sediment accretion)

acquisition.vessel: R/V SONNE (SPOC SO161, 2001) and legacy vessels

data.name: SPOC MCS Reflection Profiles — Chile Margin 35°–40°S (sediment accretion)
data.description: MCS reflection profiles from SPOC and legacy cruises imaging accretionary prism structure and sediment subduction processes along Chilean margin 35°–40°S; PhD thesis data at OceanRep GEOMAR

_data_note: PhD thesis at GEOMAR Kiel; openly accessible via OceanRep GEOMAR. SPOC SO161 and legacy cruise data.

processing.summary: MCS reflection and refraction data from SPOC and legacy cruises along the Chilean margin (35°–40°S) are analyzed to characterize the accretionary prism structure, sediment input to the trench, and the balance between sediment accretion and subduction. PhD thesis work at GEOMAR Kiel.
processing.notes: PhD thesis (GEOMAR/CAU Kiel); openly accessible via OceanRep GEOMAR. Uses SPOC SO161 and legacy Chilean margin seismic data.

analysis_notes: PhD thesis at GEOMAR Kiel; open access via OceanRep (https://oceanrep.geomar.de/id/eprint/23483/). SPOC and legacy data.
